questions.q:

$\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}+\mathrm{HCl} \rightleftharpoons \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}_2^{+}+\mathrm{Cl}^{-}$
The correct statement among the following are

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The pH of which solutions do not change with dilution?
questions.solution: pH of $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COONa}$ solution changes on dilution because it is a salt solution where as other are buffer solutions.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: To 100 ml of an aqueous solution of $0.1 \mathrm{M} \mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COOH}\left(\mathrm{Ka}=2 \times 10^{-5}\right), 0.01 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $\mathrm{HCl}(\mathrm{g})$ is passed. Select correct options regarding the resulting solution.
options: Degree of dissociation of acetic acid in resulting solution is $10^{-4}$
pH of resulting solution is nearly 1
Degree of dissociation of water in resulting solution is $1.8 \times 10^{-15}$
Concentration of $\mathrm{OH}^{-}$ions contributed by water is resulting solution is $10^{-7} \mathrm{M}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Which of the following solution will be acidic
questions.solution: In case of B, C and D, the salt are of weak base and strong acid which undergo hydrolysis to give acidic solutions $\mathrm{CH}_3 \mathrm{COONa}$ however, on hydrolysis gives basic solution.
